cards.text: Scoped enumerations are defined with {{c1::enum class}} or {{c2::enum struct}}: enum class Color {{c3::{ red, green };}}}

cards.front: What advantages do scoped enumerations have over unscoped?
cards.back:
• No namespace pollution (enumerators scoped to enum)
  • No implicit conversion to int
  • Stronger type safety
  • Can be forward declared

cards.text: To access scoped enumerator: Color{{c1::::}}red (using {{c2::scope resolution operator}})

cards.question: What's wrong?
cards.code:
enum class Color { red, green };
Color c = red;
cards.answer: Error: must use Color::red, not just red.
cards.explanation: Scoped enums require scope resolution operator.

cards.front: Do scoped enumerators implicitly convert to int?

cards.question: What's wrong?
cards.code:
enum class Color { red, green };
std::cout << Color::red;
cards.answer: Error: no implicit conversion to int for output.

cards.question: Does this compile?
cards.code:
enum class Color { red };
enum class Fruit { red };
cards.answer: Yes. Color::red and Fruit::red don't conflict (different scopes).

cards.text: {{c1::enum class}} and {{c2::enum struct}} are functionally identical.

cards.front: Can enum class be forward declared?
cards.back: Yes, without specifying base type: enum class Color;

cards.concept: Prefer enum class in modern C++
cards.explanation:
Use enum class by default:
  • Prevents naming conflicts
  • Stronger type safety (no implicit conversions)
  • More explicit and self-documenting
  • Only downside: slightly more verbose

cards.text: To specify underlying type: enum class Color : {{c1::uint8_t}} { red, green };
Scoped enumerations are defined with {{c1::enum class}} or {{c2::enum struct}}, and both are {{c3::functionally equivalent}}.
To specify the underlying type of an enum: enum class Color : {{c1::uint8_t}} { red, green };
Scoped enum comparison: Different enum class types {{c1::cannot}} be compared, providing {{c2::type safety}}.
Scoped enumerations are defined with {{c1::enum class}} or {{c2::enum struct}}, and both are {{c3::functionally equivalent}}.
To specify the underlying type of an enum: enum class Color : {{c1::uint8_t}} { red, green };
